skill.name: baoyu-xhs-images
skill.description: Creates Xiaohongshu and RedNote infographic series from articles or pasted content, with selectable visual styles and layouts.
skill.summary: Turn written content into Xiaohongshu-style infographic series with style and layout guidance.
skill.author: JimLiu

security_audit.summary: The static weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and external-command findings are false positives from markdown text, markdown file names, and fenced bash usage examples. No evidence found of executable scripts, network calls, environment access, shell invocation, or data exfiltration. One medium content-safety concern remains because a generation prompt tells the downstream image model not to refuse certain sensitive or copyrighted-figure requests.

medium_findings.title: Downstream Safety Refusal Override Wording
medium_findings.description: The base prompt says to create alternatives for sensitive or copyrighted figures and adds "DO NOT refuse to generate". This is not malware, but it can pressure an image generation model to bypass normal safety refusals or copyright safeguards.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording is directly present in a prompt used for image generation, but it also asks for stylistic alternatives rather than exact copies. The risk is moderate because misuse depends on downstream model policy handling.

content.value_statement: Long posts and articles can be hard to turn into consistent social visuals. This skill breaks content into Xiaohongshu-ready image plans with clear layouts, styles, and prompts.
actual_capabilities: Turns article files or pasted text into a planned Xiaohongshu infographic series.
Selects from nine visual styles, including cute, tech, warm, minimal, and notion.
Selects from six layout types, including sparse, dense, list, comparison, and flow.
Plans cover, content, and ending images with filenames and text content.
Creates prompt files that describe composition, colors, text, layout, and style.
Organizes outputs under an article folder or a dated direct-input output folder.
limitations: It is a planning and prompt skill, not a standalone image generation engine.
Final image quality depends on the selected image generation tool.
Dense layouts can become hard to read if the source content is too long.
Brand compliance, copyright checks, and platform policy review remain user responsibilities.

use_cases.title: Repurpose Articles for Xiaohongshu
use_cases.description: Convert essays, notes, or posts into a multi-image RedNote carousel with a cover, content pages, and ending.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Explainers
use_cases.description: Turn product points or campaign messages into consistent infographics with a selected brand-friendly style.

use_cases.title: Design Knowledge Cards
use_cases.description: Break tutorials, frameworks, and checklists into readable visual cards for learning and sharing.

prompt_templates.title: Start from Pasted Content
prompt_templates.prompt:
/baoyu-xhs-images
Paste the article or notes that should become a Xiaohongshu image series.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Visual Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the topic needs a specific mood.
prompt_templates.prompt:
/baoyu-xhs-images --style tech
Turn this AI productivity article into a Xiaohongshu infographic series.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Layout Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the content structure is already known.
prompt_templates.prompt:
/baoyu-xhs-images --layout flow
Create a step-by-step visual series from this onboarding process.

prompt_templates.title: Control Style and Layout
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for consistent campaign output.

output_examples.input: A short article explaining five ways to reduce meeting overload.
output: A sparse cover image plan with a clear title and one visual hook.
A list-style content page with five concise meeting tips.
A closing image plan with a short reminder and consistent visual style.

output_examples.input: A product note comparing traditional spreadsheets with an AI planning tool.
output: A cover image plan that introduces the comparison topic.
A comparison layout that separates spreadsheet limits from AI planning benefits.
A final summary card that highlights the main decision point.

output_examples.input: A tutorial that explains a six-step skincare routine.
output: A warm or fresh cover image plan for the routine.
A flow layout that shows each step in order.
A simple ending card that reinforces the daily habit.

best_practices: Provide a focused source article with clear sections or bullet points.
Choose a style when brand tone or audience expectations are important.
Review generated text for readability before sending prompts to an image model.
anti_patterns: Do not pack an entire long article into one dense image.
Do not mix many visual styles inside one series without a clear reason.
Do not publish outputs without checking platform rules and copyright constraints.

faq.question: Does this skill generate final image files by itself?
faq.answer: No. It prepares outlines and prompts, then relies on an available image generation skill or tool.

faq.question: Can it work with Chinese and English content?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions say the output language should match the input content language.

faq.question: How many images does it plan?
faq.answer: It plans about two to ten images, based on content complexity and series structure.

faq.question: Which styles are available?
faq.answer: Available styles include cute, fresh, tech, warm, bold, minimal, retro, pop, and notion.

faq.question: Which layouts are available?
faq.answer: Available layouts include sparse, balanced, dense, list, comparison, and flow.

faq.question: Where does it organize output files?
faq.answer: It uses an xhs-images folder next to an article, or a dated xhs-outputs folder for pasted input.
